title: [Agent] Improved drag tool and standardized UITARS response
body:
Summary of changes to support standardized AgentResponse outputs for UITARS and enhanced drag tooling:

- Standardize UITARS loop responses to the AgentResponse format so the UI (Gradio) can clearly show thoughts and actions as distinct AgentResponse objects.
- Extend drag tooling to support multi-point drag paths (drag_path) instead of only a single start->end drag. This enables dragging along a path with more than two points for upcoming agent features.
- Add per-segment visual feedback during drag_path by capturing and annotating screenshots after each segment.
- Keep existing drag support (click, drag, scroll) intact, but ensure drag-related actions reliably trigger screenshots and content that can be consumed by the UI.
- Ensure backward compatibility for existing providers and tooling while introducing new multi-point drag capability.

Impact areas to modify (with rationale):
- UITARS loop (to align responses to AgentResponse format and to handle new drag_path action)
- UITARS computer tool (to implement drag_path and per-segment results)
- Message/AgentResponse formatting (to accommodate AgentResponse structures across providers)

If you want, I can map the exact changes to a minimal patch set per file/function next."

step_plan.summary: Standardize UITARS outputs to AgentResponse format and introduce multi-point drag_path with per-segment screenshots, while preserving existing drag actions.

steps.operation: Refactor UITARSLoop.run to emit AgentResponse-like payloads and initialize drag_path handling
steps.reason: To start emitting standardized AgentResponse payloads (Thoughts and Actions) and plumb new drag_path handling into the main loop so the UI (Gradio) can render thoughts and actions as distinct AgentResponse objects and support multi-point drags.
steps.side_effects: May alter the asynchronous loop semantics; requires tests to verify AgentResponse shape and backward compatibility with existing responses.

steps.operation: Enhance UITARSLoop._handle_response to parse new drag_path actions and capture per-segment screenshots
steps.reason: To process multi-point drag_path actions and trigger per-segment screenshot capture, storing these visuals in the ToolResult content for UI consumption.
steps.side_effects: Could impact error handling and existing action parsing; needs tests for drag_path parsing and per-segment visuals.

steps.operation: Update UITARSLoop.to_uitars_format to preserve backward compatibility while exposing drag_path metadata
steps.reason: To keep UI-facing prompt formatting compatible with existing Gradio expectations while reserving metadata for drag_path handling so downstream components can render path-based actions.
steps.side_effects: May require UI rendering adjustments and regression tests for non-drag actions.

steps.operation: Add drag_path handling in BaseComputerTool.__call__ to perform multi-point drag and accumulate per-segment screenshots
steps.reason: To implement a new action 'drag_path' that traverses a list of points, executes segmented drags, and collects per-segment screenshots into ToolResult.content, enabling multi-point drag features end-to-end.
steps.side_effects: Increases complexity of core tool; ensure backward compatibility for existing actions; needs tests for multi-point drag execution and content aggregation.

steps.operation: Extend ComputerTool.to_params to accept drag_path path data via tool_input while preserving existing parameters
steps.reason: To pass a multi-point drag path to the core tool in a backward-compatible way, using path data in tool_input or parameters without breaking existing interfaces.
steps.side_effects: Parameter validation updates may be required; ensure existing consumers remain functional; update tests for drag_path parameter propagation.

steps.operation: Enhance ComputerTool.initialize_dimensions to robustly handle multi-point drags by ensuring consistent coordinate framing
steps.reason: To ensure width/height initialization reliably supports multi-point drag coordinates and consistent screen-space dragging across segments.
steps.side_effects: Coordinate calculations may affect UI alignment and test coverage; update tests to cover path-based dragging scenarios.
